sections.content:

1. Left panel: charcoal "C" shape at rows 16-19, cols 22-25 (open at right)
2. Right panel:

   - Bottom yellow shape: rows 24-27, cols 45-48 (a "U" shape, 14 pixels)
The charcoal "C" shape has 14 pixels, same as the bottom yellow "U" shape. The top yellow shape has 16 pixels.
So the goal might be to match the charcoal shape's position and orientation to one of the yellow shapes. Since the charcoal shape has 14 pixels (same as the bottom yellow "U" shape), I need to:
1. Rotate the charcoal shape to a "U" orientation (click right small square)
2. Position it to match the bottom yellow "U" shape's position
But wait, the charcoal shape is on the left panel and the yellow shapes are on the right panel. They're in different areas. Maybe the goal is to make the charcoal shape match the position within its own panel to where the yellow shape is in the right panel.
Let me think about the grid structure. The right panel has a checkerboard pattern with cells at specific positions. The yellow shapes are placed in certain cells. The left panel might have a similar grid, and I need to position the charcoal shape in the corresponding cell.
Looking at the right panel:
- Top yellow shape: rows 7-11, cols 44-49 (centered around row 9, col 46-47)
- Bottom yellow shape: rows 24-27, cols 45-48 (centered around row 25-26, col 46-47)
The left panel's charcoal shape is at rows 16-19, cols 22-25 initially. When I click the right small square, it moves to rows 8-11, cols 14-17 and rotates to a "U" shape.
So the left panel has a grid of cells, and the charcoal shape can be positioned in different cells. The buttons might control which cell the shape occupies.
Let me look at the grid structure more carefully. The right panel's checkerboard has cells at:
- Rows 4-7, 8-11, 12-15, 16-19, 20-23, 24-27, 28-31 (7 rows of cells)
- Cols 33-36, 37-40, 41-44, 45-48, 49-52, 53-56, 57-60 (7 columns of cells)
Wait, that doesn't quite work. Let me look at the charcoal nodes in the right panel:
- 16-pixel nodes: 4x4 squares
- 12-pixel nodes: 3x4 or 4x3 rectangles
The right panel seems to have a grid of cells, each cell being 4x4 pixels. The charcoal nodes represent the cells in the grid. The yellow shapes occupy certain cells.
For the left panel, the charcoal shape is at rows 16-19, cols 22-25 initially. This is a 4x4 cell at row index 4 (rows 16-19) and column index 5 (cols 22-25) within the left panel's grid.
Wait, the left panel is cols 0-31, and the right panel is cols 32-63. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the ASCII output, the left panel seems to be cols 0-31 (gray background) and the right panel is cols 32-63 (checkerboard). The charcoal shape in the left panel is at cols 22-25, which is near the right edge of the left panel.
When I click the right small square, the charcoal shape moves to rows 8-11, cols 14-17. This is a different cell in the left panel's grid.
So the left panel has a grid of cells, and the charcoal shape can be positioned in different cells. The small squares control the shape's orientation, and the buttons control its position.
But I haven't found how the buttons control the position yet. Let me try a different approach: maybe the buttons on the right panel control the position of the charcoal shape on the left panel.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the initial state. The left panel has the charcoal shape at rows 16-19, cols 22-25. The right panel has two yellow shapes. The buttons are below each panel.
Maybe the goal is to use the right panel's buttons to control the charcoal shape's position on the left panel, to match one of the yellow shapes' positions.
Let me try clicking on the right panel's buttons to see if they affect the charcoal shape's position.
Actually, I've been spending too many actions exploring. Let me try a more systematic approach: try clicking on each of the right panel's vertical bars and see what happens to the charcoal shape.
But first, let me switch back to the right small square to get the "U" orientation, since that's what the bottom yellow shape has.
